ordering: series entries are sorted by recommended_order ascending

readiness_categories.already_exists: The repo already provides the family in a materially usable form; later work should preserve or consolidate it rather than create it from zero.
readiness_categories.partially_exists: The repo contains strong precursor surfaces, but the family is still incomplete or fragmented.
readiness_categories.foundation_ready: The needed groundwork is strong enough that the family can be formalized next without greenfield invention.
readiness_categories.blocked: One or more prerequisite foundations are still missing, so execution would stall or mis-shape the architecture.
readiness_categories.dangerous: Execution now would likely freeze the wrong boundaries or semantics even if precursor surfaces exist.
readiness_categories.obsolete: The family as originally imagined is wasteful because the repo already solved the baseline problem.
readiness_categories.waits_for_lambda: The family depends on Λ-series semantic grounding before it can be frozen safely.
readiness_categories.waits_for_quarantine_review: The family depends on explicit review of a known P-2 quarantine or split-ownership zone.

series.readiness_summary: Λ is the most ready major series. The repo already embodies broad semantic and world roots, and the missing work is primarily formalization, vocabulary freezing, and cross-domain mapping rather than invention.
existing_repo_support: Broad domain and reality roots are already live.
Pack-backed law and experience surfaces already exist.
P-2 classified the semantic roots as extend, not replace.
blockers: No hard blocker for initial Λ work.
Final bridge semantics must respect the field/fields, schema/schemas, and packs/data/packs conflicts.
dangerous_assumptions: Assuming Λ needs a clean-room ontology.
Silently choosing winners in the field or pack ownership splits.
series.notes: P-4 should treat Λ as the first execution-grade series after Ρ.

series.readiness_summary: Σ has a strong existing baseline, but the parts that bind natural language, task classes, and wrappers to governed semantics should inherit Λ rather than racing ahead of it.
existing_repo_support: The agent governance baseline already exists in AGENTS and planning docs.
XStack and ControlX already expose routed execution, session, and refusal surfaces.
P-2 classified mirrored governance docs as merge and the authority stack as keep.
blockers: Canonical task and wrapper vocabularies should not be frozen before Λ stabilizes capability and domain semantics.
dangerous_assumptions: Binding natural-language task classes before capability surfaces are frozen.
Creating vendor-specific wrappers before vendor-neutral mirrors exist.
quarantine_dependencies: Any task catalog entry that hard-pins disputed field, schema, or pack semantics.
series.notes: Low-risk governance consolidation can follow immediately after initial Λ framing, but not before.

series.readiness_summary: Φ has significant live substrate, but execution is still gated by Λ semantics and Σ governance. Early Φ should extract and formalize what exists, while explicit module-loader, lifecycle, snapshot-service, render-device, and distributed-authority work remains blocked or dangerous.
existing_repo_support: Kernel and runtime substrate already exist across engine, game, app, compat, control, core, process, and server roots.
Event-log, replay, drift, checkpoint, migration, and sandbox precursors already exist.
P-2 classified the thin runtime root as replace and the substrate cluster as extend.
blockers: No explicit framegraph or render-device abstraction.
dangerous_assumptions: Assuming runtime/ is already the canonical orchestrator home.
Freezing component or state boundaries before Λ semantic grounding.
Treating shard or replay precursors as distributed-authority readiness.
series.notes: The first safe Φ move is extraction and boundary freezing, not greenfield replacement.

series.readiness_summary: Υ is mostly a consolidation series. The repo already has a strong control-plane, build, and identity substrate, but execution should follow the earliest Φ boundary decisions so versioning, release contracts, and publication policy attach to the right runtime and product surfaces.
existing_repo_support: Build and CI backbone is already keep-classified.
Build identity, artifact identity, release channel, rollback, yanked, and archive-policy surfaces already exist.
blockers: The named constitutions for versioning and release contract profiles are not yet frozen.
Full execution should inherit the Φ-facing runtime boundary map rather than guessing product/runtime attachment points.
dangerous_assumptions: Treating version labels as machine truth.
Collapsing suite version, product version, protocol/schema versions, compatibility identity, and build identity into one field.
Promoting derived build or artifact echoes into canonical authority.
quarantine_dependencies: generated output roots such as build and artifacts
series.notes: The exact docs named in the latest versioning doctrine are absent under those names, but the underlying identity and release substrate already exists.

series.readiness_summary: Ζ remains heavily gated. The repo has rollback, yanked-release, replay, checkpoint, proof, and transaction precursors, but most live cutover, hot-reload, non-blocking save, trust-rotation, and distributed-authority capabilities are still blocked or dangerous.
existing_repo_support: Rollback transaction selection and yanked release handling already exist.
Replay, checkpoint, dispute-bundle, proof, and drift surfaces already exist.
Blueprint readiness docs still classify most advanced Ζ capabilities as requiring new foundation or unrealistic.
blockers: Distributed authority semantics are not frozen.
dangerous_assumptions: Treating rollback primitives as full live-ops readiness.
Treating shard tests or checkpoint bundles as distributed-authority proof.
Planning hot swap or live reload before Φ and Υ foundations land.
series.notes: P-4 should keep Ζ narrow, heavily gated, and explicitly downstream of Φ and Υ.
